repo_purpose: p-event makes handling events easier by promisifying event results, simplifying async operations in Node.js and browsers.
architecture_summary: The repository consists primarily of event handling tools that leverage the promisified outcomes of events to enable asynchronous operations in both Node.js environments and web browsers. The primary files include a `package.json` for defining project metadata, dependencies, and scripts, and a `readme.md` for usage instructions and API details. The GitHub workflows are housed in the `.github` directory, utilizing YAML configurations to manage CI/CD pipelines, particularly for Node.js applications.

modules.name: Project Metadata
modules.responsibility: Holds project configuration including metadata, dependencies, and scripts.

modules.name: Documentation
modules.responsibility: Provides usage instructions and API details for users.

modules.name: GitHub Workflows
modules.responsibility: Defines the CI workflow for Node.js projects, facilitating testing on multiple Node versions.

risks.description: Possible contradictions between the purpose of the repository ('for Node.js and browsers') and the CI workflow ('only specified Node.js environments').
